# Transcrição: How_the_Meta_Ad_Auction_Works

- **Fonte:** How_the_Meta_Ad_Auction_Works
- **Duração:** 07:56

## [00:00 - 03:02]

This is a pubcast shot. I'm John Loomer This is how the ad auction works Let's go So if you're an advertiser who wants to be a master of your craft you need to know how everything works Well, so much has changed about meta advertising over the years The ad auction has been at its core from the beginning now It's important to separate the ad auction from the other elements. So we're not talking about ad Optimization here where you choose a performance goal and meta finds the people most likely to perform that action We're also not talking about meta-endromeda Which is what everyone is talking about right now and so few advertisers understand what it is Andromeda is a new retrieval engine that attempts to find a pool of ads to show to someone Now those two elements are absolutely important and you could argue that they are Enhancements on top of the ad auction system. They make the auction infinitely more effective so think of it this way optimization and Andromeda Decide which ads get into the auction and the auction decides which ad is shown So when you have hundreds Thousands or even millions of ads that are eligible to be shown to a single person The auction determines which ad wins and gets shown to understand meta's ad auction You need to break down its three most important elements First is the bid so when you think of an auction You surely think of an auctioneer and an audience of bidders in that traditional example The auctioned item will go to the person with the highest bid now bids are part of meta advertising too but it's happening in the background bidding is dynamic and Meta bids what is necessary to help you win the auctions and get your ads shown That doesn't mean that is gonna over bid the other elements impact what that bid needs to be now There is a manual element to this as well But that was much more prevalent in the old days of advertising But if you want more control you can set minimum or maximum bids or other constraints like cost per result goals To help guide meta in the auction But the bottom line here is that the meta ad auction isn't your traditional auction The highest bidder isn't necessarily going to be the winner now if that were the case Advertisers with the worst ads and the most money could dominate the inventory That would not only create a terrible user experience

## [03:02 - 06:03]

But the ads would likely be far too expensive than their worth now You also don't want to go the other end of this and be the lowest bidder Since you're likely to get only the lowest quality people Seeing your ads the people who advertisers aren't fighting over to get their ads shown So the bid is number one another element is Estimated action rates meta estimates the likelihood that a given person Will perform the action that you're wanting so if you're optimizing for purchases That's gonna look at signals like past purchases add to carts or time spent on product pages It uses that to predict how likely this person is to buy Now this is where we start dipping into delivery and optimization territory Let's say you have two ads competing in the auction to be shown to a single person One ad bids twice as much as the other But the ad with a lower bid also has a much higher Estimated action rate meta is likely to favor the ad that is more likely to get the action that the advertiser wants All right, so that's the second estimated action rate the third and final element is add quality Now this is determined by user feedback But there are also automated processes that help determine the quality of your ad low quality ads Are those that would withhold information use sensationalized language Use engagement bait and click bait and all those other scammy tactics Now while user feedback is a part of it. Don't worry about things like unfair reports I know that's a common concern Meta doesn't rely only on information that comes directly from users Automated systems can flag patterns in your ads and landing pages before they cause major problems If people click and bounce immediately, that's a signal too So understand that these three elements work together very closely to impact your ad costs It's your bid estimated action rates and add quality If you create low quality ads that fail to attract the actions you want from your ideal audience You will need to spend more And meta may punish advertisers who consistently create low quality ads By forcing them to spend more in the auctions So here's the bottom of the glass Winning the ad auction is more than simply having the highest bid The reality is that you don't want to be the highest bidder in most cases You also don't want to be the lowest bidder and really most advertisers shouldn't even bother worrying about bids in the first place Let meta do it dynamically for you to win the ad auction Your primary focus should be on creating quality ads

## [06:03 - 07:56]

That provide a quality user experience and attract the action you want from your target audience Of course your target audience isn't necessarily defined by you these days due to algorithmic targeting But it's your ideal audience you want your ad to attract If you're creating high quality ads that attract the action that you want from your ideal audience Guess what meta doesn't need to bid as much to show your ad to that group So here's the most important point the better your ad the more meta will favor you over higher bids So don't obsess over manipulating bids Obsess over making ads people actually want to respond to That's how you win the auction you keep costs down and you run profitable campaigns Thanks again so much for joining me on the pubcast I really do appreciate it and if you're a regular or you want to become a regular There are a couple things you can do so first up no matter where you are whether it's apple podcast spotify On my website somewhere else make sure that you subscribe rate and review this More than anything else will help me grow and I'd truly appreciate it second Subscribe to podcast alerts when you do I will notify you every time I've published a new episode It will include a brief explanation a brief summary of what was discussed in a link to my website Where that podcast and transcript are embedded to subscribe go to john loomer dot com podcast either way, I appreciate you. Thanks so much again for joining me until next time do awesome things i'm out
